page.title: glaucoma — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for glaucoma: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for glaucoma: 169 alterations and 339 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Effect of yoga on intra-ocular pressure in patients with glaucoma.
Evaluation of the nutraceutical Palmitoylethanolamide in reducing intraocular pressure (IOP) in patients with glaucoma or ocular hypertension.
Dietary intake of vitamin A and C showed a beneficial association with open-angle glaucoma.
Flavonoids have a promising role in improving visual function in patients with glaucoma and ocular hypertension.
A combination of bilberry and pycnogenol reduces intraocular hypertension and may lower the risk of symptomatic glaucoma.
A higher intake of certain fruits and vegetables may be associated with a decreased risk of glaucoma.
Additional treatment with citicoline eyedrops to IOP-lowering treatment might reduce disease progression in patients with progressing glaucoma.
Ascorbic acid metabolites are involved in intraocular pressure control in the general population.
Association of dietary intake of B vitamins with glaucoma.
Auricular acupressure lowers intraocular pressure in patients with glaucoma.
Black currant anthocyanins caused a normalization of serum levels of ET-1 and may slow down glaucoma progression.
Citicoline: A candidate for adjunct treatment of multiple sclerosis.
Curcumin has therapeutic value in the treatment of recurrent anterior uveitis of different etiologies.
Delta-9-THC, a compound found within cannabis, at the right dosage can lower intraocular pressure.
Dietary nutrient intake was associated with open-angle glaucoma independent of intraocular pressure.
Effect of an oral supplementation with a formula containing R-lipoic acid in glaucoma patients.
Effect of palmitoylethanolamide on inner retinal function in glaucoma.
Effects of French maritime pine bark/bilberry fruit extracts on intraocular pressure for primary open-angle glaucoma.
Eggplant may have therapeutic value in the treatment of diseases of the eye involving intraocular hypertension.
Evidence of the neuroprotective role of citicoline in glaucoma patients.
Garcinia kola 0.5% aqueous eye drops were comparable to Timolol 0.5% eye drops in lowering intraocular pressure.
Ginkgo biloba appears to improve preexisting visual field damage in some patients with normal tension glaucoma (NTG).
Ginkgo biloba extract and bilberry anthocyanins improve visual function in patients with normal tension glaucoma.
Ginkgo biloba improves ocular blood flow, indicating its potential therapeutic benefits in the treatment of glaucoma.
Higher levels of niacin intake were associated with decreased odds of glaucoma.

Homocysteine levels in the tear fluid and plasma of patients with primary open-angle glaucoma is often elevated, indicating that inadequate vitamin b levels (e.g. b6, b12, and folate) may contribute to the disease.
Immediate effect of yoga exercises for eyes on the macular thickness.
Intake of the ProVens dietary supplement could improve the control of intraocular pressure, particularly in patients with ocular hypertension.
Mindfulness meditation reduces intraocular pressure, lowers stress biomarkers and modulates gene expression in glaucoma.
Oral aqueous saffron extract seems to exert an ocular hypotensive effect in primary open-angle glaucoma.
Oral citicoline treatment improves visual pathway function in glaucoma.
Palmitoylethanolamide reduces intraocular pressure and improves visual field indices in individuals affected by normal-tension glaucoma.
PM2.5 was an independent factor associated with open-angle glaucoma.
Primary open-angle glaucoma patients have reduced blood levels of DHA and EPA.
Pupillary responses to high-irradiance blue light correlate with glaucoma severity.
Short-term and long-term exposure to PM10 was associated with the incidence of childhood glaucoma.
Supplementation with citicoline might significantly slow down glaucomatous rates of progression.
The effect of dietary antioxidant supplementation in patients with glaucoma.
The effect of oral citicoline and docosahexaenoic acid on the visual field of Patients with glaucoma.
The neuroprotective role of citicoline treatment in glaucoma.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive optic nerve damage without treatment; average visual field loss 0.5-1.0 dB/year; blindness in 10-20% without treatment; no spontaneous recovery of lost vision
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: IOP-lowering drops: 20-30% IOP reduction; OHTS trial showed 50% risk reduction for glaucoma development in ocular hypertension; SLT (selective laser trabeculoplasty): IOP reduction 25-30%, equivalent to drops at 3 years (LiGHT trial); trabeculectomy: 35-45% IOP reduction, superior to drops in advanced disease
remission.gap_size: Moderate — IOP control is achievable but compliance is the dominant barrier; neuroprotection remains an unmet need
remission.barrier_detail: IOP-lowering prevents further damage but cannot restore lost vision; adherence to drop therapy is poor (50-70% of patients non-adherent at 1 year); SLT (laser) as first-line (recommended by LiGHT trial) not universally adopted; once-yearly drop (latanoprostene bunod) and sustained-release implants (Durysta) improving adherence
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — bimatoprost SR implant (Durysta) expanding to real-world use; 2021 Santen DE-117 sustained-release technology; 2019 rho kinase inhibitor (netarsudil/Rhopressa) approved — new mechanism; prostaglandin analogs as first-line since late 1990s; LiGHT trial 2019 established SLT as superior first-line to drops
remission.outdated_treatments: Timolol as first-line monotherapy in patients with cardiovascular or respiratory contraindications (still common despite contraindications); brimonidine in children <5 years (CNS depression risk); aggressive IOP target without considering optic nerve status and progression rate
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Selective laser trabeculoplasty (SLT) as first-line; aerobic exercise (reduces IOP 2-3 mmHg, possibly neuroprotective); caffeine avoidance in normal-tension glaucoma; ginkgo biloba (small RCTs showing visual field stabilisation in NTG); mitochondria-targeted neuroprotection
remission.frontier_signal: ONS-5010 (intravitreal bevacizumab) for neovascular glaucoma; siRNA-based IOP reduction (monthly or quarterly dosing); stem cell-based trabecular meshwork regeneration; neuroprotective agents (CNTF, memantine — multiple Phase 3 trials failed; mechanism still being pursued); gene therapy for MYOC-associated glaucoma
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
